# solution.py
"""CollaborateCraft - a single-file social-networking app for crafters and DIY makers.

Implements profiles, project posts, group projects, comments with voting,
direct and group messaging, and search, with a thread-safe in-memory store.
"""

import copy
import os
import threading
import unittest
from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum
from typing import Callable, Dict, List, Optional, Set, Tuple


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enums
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class MediaType(Enum):
    """Type of an uploaded media item."""
    PHOTO = "photo"
    VIDEO = "video"


class Category(Enum):
    """Closed vocabulary of crafting categories used as post and project tags."""
    KNITTING = "knitting"
    WOODWORKING = "woodworking"
    PAINTING = "painting"
    SEWING = "sewing"
    POTTERY = "pottery"
    JEWELRY = "jewelry"
    ORIGAMI = "origami"
    EMBROIDERY = "embroidery"
    CROCHETING = "crocheting"
    SCRAPBOOKING = "scrapbooking"
    OTHER = "other"


class TaskStatus(Enum):
    """Lifecycle status of a group-project task."""
    TODO = "todo"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    DONE = "done"


class VoteType(Enum):
    """Direction of a vote cast on a comment."""
    UP = "up"
    DOWN = "down"


class CommentTarget(Enum):
    """Kind of entity a comment is attached to."""
    POST = "post"
    GROUP_PROJECT = "group_project"


class MessageScope(Enum):
    """Delivery scope of a message."""
    DIRECT = "direct"
    GROUP = "group"


class InviteStatus(Enum):
    """Status of a group-project invitation."""
    PENDING = "pending"
    ACCEPTED = "accepted"
    DECLINED = "declined"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Exception hierarchy
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class CollaborateCraftError(Exception):
    """Base class for all CollaborateCraft errors; never raised directly."""


class ValidationError(CollaborateCraftError):
    """Raised when input is malformed or violates a business rule."""


class NotFoundError(CollaborateCraftError):
    """Raised when a referenced entity id is unknown."""


class DuplicateError(CollaborateCraftError):
    """Raised when a uniqueness constraint is violated."""


class AuthorizationError(CollaborateCraftError):
    """Raised when the caller lacks permission for an operation."""


class ConflictError(CollaborateCraftError):
    """Raised when an operation is invalid for the current entity state."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Value object
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@dataclass
class MediaItem:
    """A single uploaded media file with its declared type."""
    filename: str
    data: bytes
    media_type: MediaType


_PHOTO_EXTENSIONS = {".jpg", ".jpeg", ".png", ".gif", ".webp"}
_VIDEO_EXTENSIONS = {".mp4", ".avi", ".mov", ".mkv", ".webm"}


def _validate_media_item(item: MediaItem) -> None:
    """Validate that a MediaItem has non-empty data and an extension matching its type."""
    if len(item.data) == 0:
        raise ValidationError("Media item data must be non-empty.")
    ext = os.path.splitext(item.filename)[1].lower()
    if item.media_type == MediaType.PHOTO:
        allowed = _PHOTO_EXTENSIONS
    elif item.media_type == MediaType.VIDEO:
        allowed = _VIDEO_EXTENSIONS
    else:
        raise ValidationError("Unknown media type.")
    if ext not in allowed:
        raise ValidationError(
            "Extension '%s' is not valid for media type %s." % (ext, item.media_type.value)
        )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entity models
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@dataclass
class User:
    """A registered CollaborateCraft user profile."""
    id: int
    username: str
    bio: str
    profile_picture: Optional[MediaItem]
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Post:
    """A project post with media, description, and category tags."""
    id: int
    author_id: int
    title: str
    description: str
    media: List[MediaItem]
    tags: Set[Category]
    created_at: datetime
    updated_at: datetime


@dataclass
class GroupProject:
    """A collaborative project led by one user with members, tasks, and invites."""
    id: int
    leader_id: int
    title: str
    description: str
    media: List[MediaItem]
    tags: Set[Category]
    member_ids: Set[int]
    task_ids: List[int]
    invite_ids: List[int]
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Task:
    """A unit of work within a group project."""
    id: int
    group_project_id: int
    title: str
    description: str
    assignee_id: Optional[int]
    status: TaskStatus
    created_at: datetime
    updated_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Comment:
    """A comment attached to a post or group project."""
    id: int
    author_id: int
    target_type: CommentTarget
    target_id: int
    content: str
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Message:
    """A direct or group message."""
    id: int
    sender_id: int
    recipient_id: Optional[int]
    group_project_id: Optional[int]
    scope: MessageScope
    content: str
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Invite:
    """An invitation for a user to join a group project."""
    id: int
    group_project_id: int
    inviter_id: int
    invitee_id: int
    status: InviteStatus
    created_at: datetime
    responded_at: Optional[datetime]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Internal infrastructure
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _IdCounter:
    """Monotonic, globally-unique id generator starting at 1."""

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self._next = 1

    def next(self) -> int:
        """Return the current id then increment; must be called under the shared lock."""
        value = self._next
        self._next += 1
        return value


@dataclass
class _Store:
    """Shared infrastructure injected into every service."""
    lock: "threading.RLock"
    counter: _IdCounter
    now: Callable[[], datetime]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# UserService
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class UserService:
    """Manages user profiles and username uniqueness."""

    def __init__(self, store: _Store) -> None:
        self._store = store
        self._users: Dict[int, User] = {}
        self._username_index: Dict[str, int] = {}

    def _require_user(self, user_id: int) -> User:
        """Return the internal User or raise NotFoundError."""
        user = self._users.get(user_id)
        if user is None:
            raise NotFoundError("User %r not found." % (user_id,))
        return user

    def register_user(
        self,
        username: str,
        bio: str = "",
        profile_picture: Optional[MediaItem] = None,
    ) -> User:
        """Create and store a new user, returning a deep copy."""
        with self._store.lock:
            if username is None or username.strip() == "":
                raise ValidationError("Username must be non-empty.")
            if username in self._username_index:
                raise DuplicateError("Username %r already registered." % (username,))
            if profile_picture is not None:
                if profile_picture.media_type != MediaType.PHOTO:
                    raise ValidationError("Profile picture must be a PHOTO.")
                _validate_media_item(profile_picture)
            stored_bio = "" if bio is None else bio
            uid = self._store.counter.next()
            user = User(
                id=uid,
                username=username,
                bio=stored_bio,
                profile_picture=copy.deepcopy(profile_picture),
                created_at=self._store.now(),
            )
            self._users[uid] = user
            self._username_index[username] = uid
            return copy.deepcopy(user)

    def get_user(self, user_id: int) -> User:
        """Return a deep copy of the user or raise NotFoundError."""
        with self._store.lock:
            return copy.deepcopy(self._require_user(user_id))

    def update_profile(
        self,
        user_id: int,
        bio: Optional[str] = None,
        profile_picture: Optional[MediaItem] = None,
    ) -> User:
        """Update bio and/or profile picture; None arguments are no-ops."""
        with self._store.lock:
            user = self._require_user(user_id)
            if profile_picture is not None:
                if profile_picture.media_type != MediaType.PHOTO:
                    raise ValidationError("Profile picture must be a PHOTO.")
                _validate_media_item(profile_picture)
            if bio is not None:
                user.bio = bio
            if profile_picture is not None:
                user.profile_picture = copy.deepcopy(profile_picture)
            return copy.deepcopy(user)

    def upload_profile_picture(self, user_id: int, media_item: MediaItem) -> User:
        """Replace the user's profile picture, enforcing PHOTO type."""
        with self._store.lock:
            user = self._require_user(user_id)
            if media_item.media_type != MediaType.PHOTO:
                raise ValidationError("Profile picture must be a PHOTO.")
            _validate_media_item(media_item)
            user.profile_picture = copy.deepcopy(media_item)
            return copy.deepcopy(user)

    def list_users(self) -> List[User]:
        """Return all users sorted by id ascending as deep copies."""
        with self._store.lock:
            ordered = [self._users[uid] for uid in sorted(self._users.keys())]
            return copy.deepcopy(ordered)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PostService
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class PostService:
    """Manages project posts and their tag/author indexes."""

    def __init__(self, store: _Store, users: UserService) -> None:
        self._store = store
        self._users = users
        self._posts: Dict[int, Post] = {}
        self._tag_index: Dict[Category, Set[int]] = {}
        self._author_index: Dict[int, Set[int]] = {}

    def _require_post(self, post_id: int) -> Post:
        """Return the internal Post or raise NotFoundError."""
        post = self._posts.get(post_id)
        if post is None:
            raise NotFoundError("Post %r not found." % (post_id,))
        return post

    def create_post(
        self,
        author_id: int,
        title: str,
        description: str,
        media: List[MediaItem] = (),
        tags: List[Category] = (),
    ) -> Post:
        """Create a new post, indexing it by tag and author, returning a deep copy."""
        with self._store.lock:
            self._users._require_user(author_id)
            if title is None or title.strip() == "":
                raise ValidationError("Post title must be non-empty.")
            if description is None or description.strip() == "":
                raise ValidationError("Post description must be non-empty.")
            media_list = list(media)
            for item in media_list:
                _validate_media_item(item)
            tag_set = set(tags)
            now = self._store.now()
            pid = self._store.counter.next()
            post = Post(
                id=pid,
                author_id=author_id,
                title=title,
                description=description,
                media=copy.deepcopy(media_list),
                tags=tag_set,
                created_at=now,
                updated_at=now,
            )
            self._posts[pid] = post
            for tag in tag_set:
                self._tag_index.setdefault(tag, set()).add(pid)
            self._author_index.setdefault(author_id, set()).add(pid)
            return copy.deepcopy(post)

    def get_post(self, post_id: int) -> Post:
        """Return a deep copy of the post or raise NotFoundError."""
        with self._store.lock:
            return copy.deepcopy(self._require_post(post_id))

    def edit_post(
        self,
        author_id: int,
        post_id: int,
        title: Optional[str] = None,
        description: Optional[str] = None,
        tags: Optional[List[Category]] = None,
    ) -> Post:
        """Edit post fields; None arguments are no-ops; returns updated deep copy."""
        with self._store.lock:
            post = self._require_post(post_id)
            self._users._require_user(author_id)
            if author_id != post.author_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("Only the author may edit this post.")
            if title is not None and title.strip() == "":
                raise ValidationError("Post title must be non-empty.")
            if description is not None and description.strip() == "":
                raise ValidationError("Post description must be non-empty.")
            if title is not None:
                post.title = title
            if description is not None:
                post.description = description
            if tags is not None:
                new_tags = set(tags)
                for tag in post.tags - new_tags:
                    bucket = self._tag_index.get(tag)
                    if bucket is not None:
                        bucket.discard(post_id)
                for tag in new_tags - post.tags:
                    self._tag_index.setdefault(tag, set()).add(post_id)
                post.tags = new_tags
            post.updated_at = self._store.now()
            return copy.deepcopy(post)

    def delete_post(self, author_id: int, post_id: int) -> None:
        """Delete a post and remove it from all indexes."""
        with self._store.lock:
            post = self._require_post(post_id)
            self._users._require_user(author_id)
            if author_id != post.author_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("Only the author may delete this post.")
            for tag in post.tags:
                bucket = self._tag_index.get(tag)
                if bucket is not None:
                    bucket.discard(post_id)
            author_bucket = self._author_index.get(post.author_id)
            if author_bucket is not None:
                author_bucket.discard(post_id)
            del self._posts[post_id]
            return None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# GroupProjectService
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class GroupProjectService:
    """Manages group projects, members, tasks, and invitations."""

    def __init__(self, store: _Store, users: UserService) -> None:
        self._store = store
        self._users = users
        self._group_projects: Dict[int, GroupProject] = {}
        self._tasks: Dict[int, Task] = {}
        self._invites: Dict[int, Invite] = {}
        self._gp_tag_index: Dict[Category, Set[int]] = {}
        self._member_index: Dict[int, Set[int]] = {}
        self._pending_invites_index: Dict[Tuple[int, int], int] = {}

    def _require_gp(self, gp_id: int) -> GroupProject:
        """Return the internal GroupProject or raise NotFoundError."""
        gp = self._group_projects.get(gp_id)
        if gp is None:
            raise NotFoundError("Group project %r not found." % (gp_id,))
        return gp

    def _require_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        """Return the internal Task or raise NotFoundError."""
        task = self._tasks.get(task_id)
        if task is None:
            raise NotFoundError("Task %r not found." % (task_id,))
        return task

    def _require_invite(self, invite_id: int) -> Invite:
        """Return the internal Invite or raise NotFoundError."""
        invite = self._invites.get(invite_id)
        if invite is None:
            raise NotFoundError("Invite %r not found." % (invite_id,))
        return invite

    def create_group_project(
        self,
        leader_id: int,
        title: str,
        description: str,
        tags: List[Category] = (),
        media: List[MediaItem] = (),
    ) -> GroupProject:
        """Create a group project with the leader as the first member."""
        with self._store.lock:
            self._users._require_user(leader_id)
            if title is None or title.strip() == "":
                raise ValidationError("Group project title must be non-empty.")
            if description is None or description.strip() == "":
                raise ValidationError("Group project description must be non-empty.")
            media_list = list(media)
            for item in media_list:
                _validate_media_item(item)
            tag_set = set(tags)
            gid = self._store.counter.next()
            gp = GroupProject(
                id=gid,
                leader_id=leader_id,
                title=title,
                description=description,
                media=copy.deepcopy(media_list),
                tags=tag_set,
                member_ids={leader_id},
                task_ids=[],
                invite_ids=[],
                created_at=self._store.now(),
            )
            self._group_projects[gid] = gp
            for tag in tag_set:
                self._gp_tag_index.setdefault(tag, set()).add(gid)
            self._member_index.setdefault(leader_id, set()).add(gid)
            return copy.deepcopy(gp)

    def get_group_project(self, group_project_id: int) -> GroupProject:
        """Return a deep copy of the group project or raise NotFoundError."""
        with self._store.lock:
            return copy.deepcopy(self._require_gp(group_project_id))

    def invite_user(
        self,
        leader_id: int,
        group_project_id: int,
        invitee_id: int,
    ) -> Invite:
        """Create a PENDING invitation for a user to join the group project."""
        with self._store.lock:
            gp = self._require_gp(group_project_id)
            self._users._require_user(leader_id)
            self._users._require_user(invitee_id)
            if leader_id != gp.leader_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("Only the leader may invite users.")
            if leader_id == invitee_id:
                raise ValidationError("Cannot invite yourself.")
            if invitee_id in gp.member_ids:
                raise ConflictError("User is already a member.")
            key = (invitee_id, group_project_id)
            if key in self._pending_invites_index:
                raise DuplicateError("A pending invite already exists for this user.")
            iid = self._store.counter.next()
            invite = Invite(
                id=iid,
                group_project_id=group_project_id,
                inviter_id=leader_id,
                invitee_id=invitee_id,
                status=InviteStatus.PENDING,
                created_at=self._store.now(),
                responded_at=None,
            )
            self._invites[iid] = invite
            gp.invite_ids.append(iid)
            self._pending_invites_index[key] = iid
            return copy.deepcopy(invite)

    def respond_to_invite(
        self,
        invitee_id: int,
        invite_id: int,
        accept: bool,
    ) -> Invite:
        """Accept or decline a pending invitation, returning the updated invite."""
        with self._store.lock:
            invite = self._require_invite(invite_id)
            if invitee_id != invite.invitee_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("Only the invitee may respond to this invite.")
            if invite.status != InviteStatus.PENDING:
                raise ConflictError("Invite has already been responded to.")
            invite.responded_at = self._store.now()
            gp = self._require_gp(invite.group_project_id)
            if accept:
                invite.status = InviteStatus.ACCEPTED
                gp.member_ids.add(invitee_id)
                self._member_index.setdefault(invitee_id, set()).add(gp.id)
            else:
                invite.status = InviteStatus.DECLINED
            self._pending_invites_index.pop((invitee_id, invite.group_project_id), None)
            return copy.deepcopy(invite)

    def create_task(
        self,
        leader_id: int,
        group_project_id: int,
        title: str,
        description: str = "",
    ) -> Task:
        """Create a TODO task within the group project."""
        with self._store.lock:
            gp = self._require_gp(group_project_id)
            self._users._require_user(leader_id)
            if leader_id != gp.leader_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("Only the leader may create tasks.")
            if title is None or title.strip() == "":
                raise ValidationError("Task title must be non-empty.")
            now = self._store.now()
            tid = self._store.counter.next()
            task = Task(
                id=tid,
                group_project_id=group_project_id,
                title=title,
                description="" if description is None else description,
                assignee_id=None,
                status=TaskStatus.TODO,
                created_at=now,
                updated_at=now,
            )
            self._tasks[tid] = task
            gp.task_ids.append(tid)
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def assign_task(
        self,
        leader_id: int,
        group_project_id: int,
        task_id: int,
        assignee_id: int,
    ) -> Task:
        """Assign or re-assign a task to a group-project member."""
        with self._store.lock:
            gp = self._require_gp(group_project_id)
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._users._require_user(assignee_id)
            if leader_id != gp.leader_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("Only the leader may assign tasks.")
            if assignee_id not in gp.member_ids:
                raise ValidationError("Assignee must be a member of the group project.")
            task.assignee_id = assignee_id
            task.updated_at = self._store.now()
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def update_task_status(
        self,
        user_id: int,
        group_project_id: int,
        task_id: int,
        status: TaskStatus,
    ) -> Task:
        """Update a task's status if called by its assignee or the leader."""
        with self._store.lock:
            gp = self._require_gp(group_project_id)
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._users._require_user(user_id)
            if user_id != task.assignee_id and user_id != gp.leader_id:
                raise AuthorizationError(
                    "Only the assignee or leader may update task status."
                )
            task.status = status
            task.updated_at = self._store.now()
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def list_members(self, group_project_id: int) -> List[User]:
        """Return deep copies of the group project's members sorted by user id."""
        with self._store.lock:
            gp = self._require_gp(group_project_id)
            members = [
                self._users._users[uid]
                for uid in sorted(gp.member_ids)
                if uid in self._users._users
            ]
            return copy.deepcopy(members)

    def get_project_progress(self, group_project_id: int) -> float:
        """Return the fraction of tasks that are DONE, in the range [0.0, 1.0]."""
        with self._store.lock:
            gp = self._require_gp(group_project_id)
            tasks = [self._tasks[tid] for tid in gp.task_ids]
            if len(tasks) == 0:
                return 0.0
            done = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.DONE)
            return done / len(tasks)

    def list_pending_invites(self, user_id: int) -> List[Invite]:
        """Return deep copies of the user's PENDING invites sorted by created_at."""
        with self._store.lock:
            self._users._require_user(user_id)
            pending = [
                inv
                for inv in self._invites.values()
                if inv.invitee_id == user_id and inv.status == InviteStatus.PENDING
            ]
            pending.sort(key=lambda inv: inv.created_at)
            return copy.deepcopy(pending)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# CommentService
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class CommentService:
    """Manages comments on posts/group projects and their votes."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        store: _Store,
        users: UserService,
        posts: PostService,
        groups: GroupProjectService,
    ) -> None:
        self._store = store
        self._users = users
        self._posts = posts
        self._groups = groups
        self._comments: Dict[int, Comment] = {}
        self._votes: Dict[int, Dict[int, VoteType]] = {}
        self._post_comments: Dict[int, List[int]] = {}
        self._gp_comments: Dict[int, List[int]] = {}

    def _require_comment(self, comment_id: int) -> Comment:
        """Return the internal Comment or raise NotFoundError."""
        comment = self._comments.get(comment_id)
        if comment is None:
            raise NotFoundError("Comment %r not found." % (comment_id,))
        return comment

    def add_comment(
        self,
        author_id: int,
        target_type: CommentTarget,
        target_id: int,
        content: str,
    ) -> Comment:
        """Add a comment to a post or group project, returning a deep copy."""
        with self._store.lock:
            self._users._require_user(author_id)
            if target_type == CommentTarget.POST:
                self._posts._require_post(target_id)
            elif target_type == CommentTarget.GROUP_PROJECT:
                gp = self._groups._require_gp(target_id)
                if author_id not in gp.member_ids:
                    raise AuthorizationError(
                        "Only members may comment on a group project."
                    )
            else:
                raise ValidationError("Unknown comment target type.")
            if content is None or content.strip() == "":
                raise ValidationError("Comment content must be non-empty.")
            cid = self._store.counter.next()
            comment = Comment(
                id=cid,
                author_id=author_id,
                target_type=target_type,
                target_id=target_id,
                content=content,
                created_at=self._store.now(),
            )
            self._comments[cid] = comment
            self._votes[cid] = {}
            if target_type == CommentTarget.POST:
                self._post_comments.setdefault(target_id, []).append(cid)
            else:
                self._gp_comments.setdefault(target_id, []).append(cid)
            return copy.deepcopy(comment)

    def _score(self, comment_id: int) -> int:
        """Return the raw score for a comment id (no validation)."""
        votes = self._votes.get(comment_id, {})
        return sum(1 if v == VoteType.UP else -1 for v in votes.values())

    def vote_comment(
        self,
        user_id: int,
        comment_id: int,
        vote_type: VoteType,
    ) -> int:
        """Cast, toggle off, or switch a vote, returning the new comment score."""
        with self._store.lock:
            self._users._require_user(user_id)
            self._require_comment(comment_id)
            votes = self._votes.setdefault(comment_id, {})
            existing = votes.get(user_id)
            if existing is None:
                votes[user_id] = vote_type
            elif existing == vote_type:
                del votes[user_id]
            else:
                votes[user_id] = vote_type
            return self._score(comment_id)

    def get_comment_score(self, comment_id: int) -> int:
        """Return count(UP) - count(DOWN) for a comment or raise NotFoundError."""
        with self._store.lock:
            self._require_comment(comment_id)
            return self._score(comment_id)

    def get_comments(
        self,
        target_type: CommentTarget,
        target_id: int,
    ) -> List[Comment]:
        """Return insertion-ordered deep copies of a target's comments."""
        with self._store.lock:
            if target_type == CommentTarget.POST:
                self._posts._require_post(target_id)
                ids = self._post_comments.get(target_id, [])
            elif target_type == CommentTarget.GROUP_PROJECT:
                self._groups._require_gp(target_id)
                ids = self._gp_comments.get(target_id, [])
            else:
                raise ValidationError("Unknown comment target type.")
            ordered = [self._comments[cid] for cid in ids]
            return copy.deepcopy(ordered)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# MessageService
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class MessageService:
    """Manages direct and group messages with conversation/inbox indexes."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        store: _Store,
        users: UserService,
        groups: GroupProjectService,
    ) -> None:
        self._store = store
        self._users = users
        self._groups = groups
        self._messages: Dict[int, Message] = {}
        self._direct_convos: Dict[frozenset, List[int]] = {}
        self._group_convos: Dict[int, List[int]] = {}
        self._inbox: Dict[int, List[int]] = {}

    def send_direct_message(
        self,
        sender_id: int,
        recipient_id: int,
        content: str,
    ) -> Message:
        """Send a direct message and index it by conversation and inbox."""
        with self._store.lock:
            self._users._require_user(sender_id)
            self._users._require_user(recipient_id)
            if sender_id == recipient_id:
                raise ValidationError("Cannot send a direct message to yourself.")
            if content is None or content.strip() == "":
                raise ValidationError("Message content must be non-empty.")
            mid = self._store.counter.next()
            message = Message(
                id=mid,
                sender_id=sender_id,
                recipient_id=recipient_id,
                group_project_id=None,
                scope=MessageScope.DIRECT,
                content=content,
                created_at=self._store.now(),
            )
            self._messages[mid] = message
            key = frozenset({sender_id, recipient_id})
            self._direct_convos.setdefault(key, []).append(mid)
            self._inbox.setdefault(recipient_id, []).append(mid)
            return copy.deepcopy(message)

    def send_group_message(
        self,
        sender_id: int,
        group_project_id: int,
        content: str,
    ) -> Message:
        """Send a message to a group project the sender is a member of."""
        with self._store.lock:
            self._users._require_user(sender_id)
            gp = self._groups._require_gp(group_project_id)
            if sender_id not in gp.member_ids:
                raise AuthorizationError("Only members may message the group project.")
            if content is None or content.strip() == "":
                raise ValidationError("Message content must be non-empty.")
            mid = self._store.counter.next()
            message = Message(
                id=mid,
                sender_id=sender_id,
                recipient_id=None,
                group_project_id=group_project_id,
                scope=MessageScope.GROUP,
                content=content,
                created_at=self._store.now(),
            )
            self._messages[mid] = message
            self._group_convos.setdefault(group_project_id, []).append(mid)
            return copy.deepcopy(message)

    def get_direct_conversation(self, user1_id: int, user2_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        """Return chronologically ordered deep copies of two users' direct messages."""
        with self._store.lock:
            self._users._require_user(user1_id)
            self._users._require_user(user2_id)
            key = frozenset({user1_id, user2_id})
            ids = self._direct_convos.get(key, [])
            ordered = [self._messages[mid] for mid in ids]
            return copy.deepcopy(ordered)

    def get_group_conversation(
        self,
        group_project_id: int,
        requester_id: int,
    ) -> List[Message]:
        """Return chronologically ordered deep copies of a group project's messages."""
        with self._store.lock:
            gp = self._groups._require_gp(group_project_id)
            self._users._require_user(requester_id)
            if requester_id not in gp.member_ids:
                raise AuthorizationError(
                    "Only members may read the group conversation."
                )
            ids = self._group_convos.get(group_project_id, [])
            ordered = [self._messages[mid] for mid in ids]
            return copy.deepcopy(ordered)

    def get_inbox(self, user_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        """Return chronologically ordered deep copies of a user's received direct messages."""
        with self._store.lock:
            self._users._require_user(user_id)
            ids = self._inbox.get(user_id, [])
            ordered = [self._messages[mid] for mid in ids]
            return copy.deepcopy(ordered)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SearchService
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class SearchService:
    """Read-only search across users, posts, and group projects."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        users: UserService,
        posts: PostService,
        groups: GroupProjectService,
        lock: "threading.RLock",
    ) -> None:
        self._users = users._users
        self._posts = posts._posts
        self._tag_index = posts._tag_index
        self._author_index = posts._author_index
        self._group_projects = groups._group_projects
        self._gp_tag_index = groups._gp_tag_index
        self._member_index = groups._member_index
        self._lock = lock

    def search_users(self, keyword: str = "") -> List[User]:
        """Return users whose username or bio contains keyword (case-insensitive)."""
        with self._lock:
            kw = (keyword or "").strip().lower()
            results = []
            for uid in sorted(self._users.keys()):
                user = self._users[uid]
                if kw == "" or kw in user.username.lower() or kw in user.bio.lower():
                    results.append(user)
            return copy.deepcopy(results)

    def search_posts(
        self,
        keyword: str = "",
        tag: Optional[Category] = None,
        author_id: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> List[Post]:
        """Return posts matching all provided keyword/tag/author criteria (AND)."""
        with self._lock:
            if author_id is not None and author_id not in self._users:
                raise NotFoundError("Author %r not found." % (author_id,))
            if tag is not None:
                candidate_ids = set(self._tag_index.get(tag, set()))
            else:
                candidate_ids = set(self._posts.keys())
            if author_id is not None:
                candidate_ids &= self._author_index.get(author_id, set())
            kw = (keyword or "").strip().lower()
            results = []
            for pid in sorted(candidate_ids):
                post = self._posts.get(pid)
                if post is None:
                    continue
                if kw != "" and kw not in post.title.lower() and kw not in post.description.lower():
                    continue
                results.append(post)
            return copy.deepcopy(results)

    def search_group_projects(
        self,
        keyword: str = "",
        tag: Optional[Category] = None,
        member_id: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> List[GroupProject]:
        """Return group projects matching all provided criteria (AND)."""
        with self._lock:
            if member_id is not None and member_id not in self._users:
                raise NotFoundError("Member %r not found." % (member_id,))
            if tag is not None:
                candidate_ids = set(self._gp_tag_index.get(tag, set()))
            else:
                candidate_ids = set(self._group_projects.keys())
            if member_id is not None:
                candidate_ids &= self._member_index.get(member_id, set())
            kw = (keyword or "").strip().lower()
            results = []
            for gid in sorted(candidate_ids):
                gp = self._group_projects.get(gid)
                if gp is None:
                    continue
                if kw != "" and kw not in gp.title.lower() and kw not in gp.description.lower():
                    continue
                results.append(gp)
            return copy.deepcopy(results)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Facade
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class CollaborateCraft:
    """Top-level facade coordinating all CollaborateCraft services under one lock."""

    def __init__(self, now: Callable[[], datetime] = datetime.now) -> None:
        self._lock = threading.RLock()
        self._counter = _IdCounter()
        self._store = _Store(lock=self._lock, counter=self._counter, now=now)
        self._users = UserService(self._store)
        self._posts = PostService(self._store, self._users)
        self._groups = GroupProjectService(self._store, self._users)
        self._comments = CommentService(self._store, self._users, self._posts, self._groups)
        self._messages = MessageService(self._store, self._users, self._groups)
        self._search = SearchService(self._users, self._posts, self._groups, self._lock)

    # --- User management ---
    def register_user(
        self,
        username: str,
        bio: str = "",
        profile_picture: Optional[MediaItem] = None,
    ) -> User:
        """Register a new user profile."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._users.register_user(username, bio, profile_picture)

    def get_user(self, user_id: int) -> User:
        """Return a user by id."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._users.get_user(user_id)

    def update_profile(
        self,
        user_id: int,
        bio: Optional[str] = None,
        profile_picture: Optional[MediaItem] = None,
    ) -> User:
        """Update a user's bio and/or profile picture."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._users.update_profile(user_id, bio, profile_picture)

    def upload_profile_picture(self, user_id: int, media_item: MediaItem) -> User:
        """Replace a user's profile picture with a PHOTO media item."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._users.upload_profile_picture(user_id, media_item)

    def list_users(self) -> List[User]:
        """List all users sorted by id ascending."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._users.list_users()

    # --- Post management ---
    def create_post(
        self,
        author_id: int,
        title: str,
        description: str,
        media: List[MediaItem] = (),
        tags: List[Category] = (),
    ) -> Post:
        """Create a new project post."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._posts.create_post(author_id, title, description, media, tags)

    def get_post(self, post_id: int) -> Post:
        """Return a post by id."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._posts.get_post(post_id)

    def edit_post(
        self,
        author_id: int,
        post_id: int,
        title: Optional[str] = None,
        description: Optional[str] = None,
        tags: Optional[List[Category]] = None,
    ) -> Post:
        """Edit an existing post owned by the author."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._posts.edit_post(author_id, post_id, title, description, tags)

    def delete_post(self, author_id: int, post_id: int) -> None:
        """Delete a post owned by the author."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._posts.delete_post(author_id, post_id)

    # --- Group project management ---
    def create_group_project(
        self,
        leader_id: int,
        title: str,
        description: str,
        tags: List[Category] = (),
        media: List[MediaItem] = (),
    ) -> GroupProject:
        """Create a new group project led by leader_id."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._groups.create_group_project(
                leader_id, title, description, tags, media
            )

    def get_group_project(self, group_project_id: int) -> GroupProject:
        """Return a group project by id."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._groups.get_group_project(group_project_id)

    def invite_user(
        self,
        leader_id: int,
        group_project_id: int,
        invitee_id: int,
    ) -> Invite:
        """Invite a user to join a group project."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._groups.invite_user(leader_id, group_project_id, invitee_id)

    def respond_to_invite(
        self,
        invitee_id: int,
        invite_id: int,
        accept: bool,
    ) -> Invite:
        """Accept or decline a pending invitation."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._groups.respond_to_invite(invitee_id, invite_id, accept)

    def create_task(
        self,
        leader_id: int,
        group_project_id: int,
        title: str,
        description: str = "",
    ) -> Task:
        """Create a task within a group project."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._groups.create_task(
                leader_id, group_project_id, title, description
            )

    def assign_task(
        self,
        leader_id: int,
        group_project_id: int,
        task_id: int,
        assignee_id: int,
    ) -> Task:
        """Assign a task to a group-project member."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._groups.assign_task(
                leader_id, group_project_id, task_id, assignee_id
            )

    def update_task_status(
        self,
        user_id: int,
        group_project_id: int,
        task_id: int,
        status: TaskStatus,
    ) -> Task:
        """Update the status of a task."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._groups.update_task_status(
                user_id, group_project_id, task_id, status
            )

    def list_members(self, group_project_id: int) -> List[User]:
        """List the members of a group project sorted by id."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._groups.list_members(group_project_id)

    def get_project_progress(self, group_project_id: int) -> float:
        """Return the fraction of DONE tasks for a group project."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._groups.get_project_progress(group_project_id)

    def list_pending_invites(self, user_id: int) -> List[Invite]:
        """List a user's pending invitations."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._groups.list_pending_invites(user_id)

    # --- Comments and voting ---
    def add_comment(
        self,
        author_id: int,
        target_type: CommentTarget,
        target_id: int,
        content: str,
    ) -> Comment:
        """Add a comment to a post or group project."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._comments.add_comment(
                author_id, target_type, target_id, content
            )

    def vote_comment(
        self,
        user_id: int,
        comment_id: int,
        vote_type: VoteType,
    ) -> int:
        """Vote on a comment and return the updated score."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._comments.vote_comment(user_id, comment_id, vote_type)

    def get_comment_score(self, comment_id: int) -> int:
        """Return the score of a comment."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._comments.get_comment_score(comment_id)

    def get_comments(
        self,
        target_type: CommentTarget,
        target_id: int,
    ) -> List[Comment]:
        """Return the comments on a post or group project in insertion order."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._comments.get_comments(target_type, target_id)

    # --- Messaging ---
    def send_direct_message(
        self,
        sender_id: int,
        recipient_id: int,
        content: str,
    ) -> Message:
        """Send a direct message to another user."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._messages.send_direct_message(sender_id, recipient_id, content)

    def send_group_message(
        self,
        sender_id: int,
        group_project_id: int,
        content: str,
    ) -> Message:
        """Send a message to a group project."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._messages.send_group_message(
                sender_id, group_project_id, content
            )

    def get_direct_conversation(self, user1_id: int, user2_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        """Return the direct conversation between two users."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._messages.get_direct_conversation(user1_id, user2_id)

    def get_group_conversation(
        self,
        group_project_id: int,
        requester_id: int,
    ) -> List[Message]:
        """Return the group conversation for a member."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._messages.get_group_conversation(group_project_id, requester_id)

    def get_inbox(self, user_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        """Return a user's received direct messages."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._messages.get_inbox(user_id)

    # --- Search ---
    def search_users(self, keyword: str = "") -> List[User]:
        """Search users by username or bio."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._search.search_users(keyword)

    def search_posts(
        self,
        keyword: str = "",
        tag: Optional[Category] = None,
        author_id: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> List[Post]:
        """Search posts by keyword, tag, and/or author."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._search.search_posts(keyword, tag, author_id)

    def search_group_projects(
        self,
        keyword: str = "",
        tag: Optional[Category] = None,
        member_id: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> List[GroupProject]:
        """Search group projects by keyword, tag, and/or member."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._search.search_group_projects(keyword, tag, member_id)

def _fixed_clock() -> datetime:
    """Return a fixed timestamp for deterministic tests."""
    return datetime(2024, 1, 1, 12, 0, 0)


def _photo(name: str = "pic.jpg", data: bytes = b"img") -> MediaItem:
    """Build a valid PHOTO media item for tests."""
    return MediaItem(filename=name, data=data, media_type=MediaType.PHOTO)


def _video(name: str = "clip.mp4", data: bytes = b"vid") -> MediaItem:
    """Build a valid VIDEO media item for tests."""
    return MediaItem(filename=name, data=data, media_type=MediaType.VIDEO)

class TestGroupProject(unittest.TestCase):

        self.leader = self.app.register_user("leader")

class TestComments(unittest.TestCase):
    def setUp(self) -> None:
        self.app = CollaborateCraft(now=_fixed_clock)
        self.author = self.app.register_user("author")
        self.voter1 = self.app.register_user("v1")
        self.voter2 = self.app.register_user("v2")
        self.post = self.app.create_post(self.author.id, "T", "D")
        self.gp = self.app.create_group_project(self.author.id, "GP", "D")

    def test_add_comment_to_post(self):
        c = self.app.add_comment(self.author.id, CommentTarget.POST, self.post.id, "hi")
        self.assertEqual(c.content, "hi")
        self.assertEqual(c.target_type, CommentTarget.POST)

    def test_add_comment_to_gp_member(self):
        c = self.app.add_comment(
            self.author.id, CommentTarget.GROUP_PROJECT, self.gp.id, "yo"
        )
        self.assertEqual(c.content, "yo")

    def test_add_comment_to_gp_non_member(self):
        with self.assertRaises(AuthorizationError):
            self.app.add_comment(
                self.voter1.id, CommentTarget.GROUP_PROJECT, self.gp.id, "no"
            )

    def test_add_comment_empty_content(self):
        with self.assertRaises(ValidationError):
            self.app.add_comment(self.author.id, CommentTarget.POST, self.post.id, "  ")

    def test_add_comment_post_not_found(self):
        with self.assertRaises(NotFoundError):
            self.app.add_comment(self.author.id, CommentTarget.POST, 999, "x")

    def test_get_comments_insertion_order(self):
        c1 = self.app.add_comment(self.author.id, CommentTarget.POST, self.post.id, "a")
        c2 = self.app.add_comment(self.author.id, CommentTarget.POST, self.post.id, "b")
        comments = self.app.get_comments(CommentTarget.POST, self.post.id)
        self.assertEqual([c.id for c in comments], [c1.id, c2.id])

    def _comment(self):
        return self.app.add_comment(
            self.author.id, CommentTarget.POST, self.post.id, "c"
        )

    def test_vote_up_from_none(self):
        c = self._comment()
        self.assertEqual(self.app.vote_comment(self.voter1.id, c.id, VoteType.UP), 1)

    def test_vote_down_from_none(self):
        c = self._comment()
        self.assertEqual(self.app.vote_comment(self.voter1.id, c.id, VoteType.DOWN), -1)

    def test_vote_toggle_up_off(self):
        c = self._comment()
        self.app.vote_comment(self.voter1.id, c.id, VoteType.UP)
        self.assertEqual(self.app.vote_comment(self.voter1.id, c.id, VoteType.UP), 0)

    def test_vote_toggle_down_off(self):
        c = self._comment()
        self.app.vote_comment(self.voter1.id, c.id, VoteType.DOWN)
        self.assertEqual(self.app.vote_comment(self.voter1.id, c.id, VoteType.DOWN), 0)

    def test_vote_switch_up_to_down(self):
        c = self._comment()
        self.app.vote_comment(self.voter1.id, c.id, VoteType.UP)
        self.assertEqual(self.app.vote_comment(self.voter1.id, c.id, VoteType.DOWN), -1)

    def test_vote_switch_down_to_up(self):
        c = self._comment()
        self.app.vote_comment(self.voter1.id, c.id, VoteType.DOWN)
        self.assertEqual(self.app.vote_comment(self.voter1.id, c.id, VoteType.UP), 1)

    def test_multiple_users_vote(self):
        c = self._comment()
        self.app.vote_comment(self.voter1.id, c.id, VoteType.UP)
        self.app.vote_comment(self.voter2.id, c.id, VoteType.UP)
        self.app.vote_comment(self.author.id, c.id, VoteType.DOWN)
        self.assertEqual(self.app.get_comment_score(c.id), 1)

    def test_get_comment_score_matches_vote_return(self):
        c = self._comment()
        ret = self.app.vote_comment(self.voter1.id, c.id, VoteType.UP)
        self.assertEqual(ret, self.app.get_comment_score(c.id))

    def test_get_comment_score_not_found(self):
        with self.assertRaises(NotFoundError):
            self.app.get_comment_score(999)

class TestEdgeCases(unittest.TestCase):
    def setUp(self) -> None:
        self.app = CollaborateCraft(now=_fixed_clock)

    def test_invalid_media_extension_mismatch(self):
        bad = MediaItem("photo.jpg", b"data", MediaType.VIDEO)
        with self.assertRaises(ValidationError):
            _validate_media_item(bad)

    def test_empty_media_data(self):
        bad = MediaItem("photo.jpg", b"", MediaType.PHOTO)
        with self.assertRaises(ValidationError):
            _validate_media_item(bad)

    def test_deep_copy_isolation_user(self):
        u = self.app.register_user("alice", "bio")
        u.bio = "changed"
        self.assertEqual(self.app.get_user(u.id).bio, "bio")

    def test_deep_copy_isolation_post(self):
        a = self.app.register_user("author")
        p = self.app.create_post(a.id, "T", "D", tags=[Category.KNITTING])
        p.tags.add(Category.POTTERY)
        self.assertNotIn(Category.POTTERY, self.app.get_post(p.id).tags)

    def test_monotonic_ids_globally_unique(self):
        u1 = self.app.register_user("u1")
        u2 = self.app.register_user("u2")
        p = self.app.create_post(u1.id, "T", "D")
        gp = self.app.create_group_project(u2.id, "G", "D")
        ids = [u1.id, u2.id, p.id, gp.id]
        self.assertEqual(ids, [1, 2, 3, 4])
        self.assertEqual(len(set(ids)), len(ids))

    def test_shared_lock_identity(self):
        lock_id = id(self.app._lock)
        self.assertEqual(id(self.app._users._store.lock), lock_id)
        self.assertEqual(id(self.app._posts._store.lock), lock_id)
        self.assertEqual(id(self.app._groups._store.lock), lock_id)
        self.assertEqual(id(self.app._comments._store.lock), lock_id)
        self.assertEqual(id(self.app._messages._store.lock), lock_id)
        self.assertEqual(id(self.app._search._lock), lock_id)

    def test_thread_safety_concurrent_register(self):
        errors = []
        results = []

        def worker(n):
            try:
                u = self.app.register_user("user%d" % n)
                results.append(u.id)
            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
                errors.append(exc)

        threads = [threading.Thread(target=worker, args=(i,)) for i in range(20)]
        for t in threads:
            t.start()
        for t in threads:
            t.join()
        self.assertEqual(errors, [])
        self.assertEqual(len(results), 20)
        self.assertEqual(len(set(results)), 20)

    def test_large_tag_search(self):
        author = self.app.register_user("bulk")
        knitting_ids = []
        for i in range(200):
            if i % 2 == 0:
                p = self.app.create_post(
                    author.id, "T%d" % i, "D", tags=[Category.KNITTING]
                )
                knitting_ids.append(p.id)
            else:
                self.app.create_post(author.id, "T%d" % i, "D", tags=[Category.PAINTING])
        results = self.app.search_posts(tag=Category.KNITTING)
        self.assertEqual([p.id for p in results], sorted(knitting_ids))
        self.assertEqual(len(results), 100)

    def test_group_project_data_consistency(self):
        leader = self.app.register_user("leader")
        gp = self.app.create_group_project(leader.id, "GP", "D")
        task_ids = [
            self.app.create_task(leader.id, gp.id, "T%d" % i).id for i in range(10)
        ]

        def worker(tid):
            self.app.update_task_status(leader.id, gp.id, tid, TaskStatus.DONE)

        threads = [threading.Thread(target=worker, args=(tid,)) for tid in task_ids]
        for t in threads:
            t.start()
        for t in threads:
            t.join()
        self.assertEqual(self.app.get_project_progress(gp.id), 1.0)
        stored = self.app.get_group_project(gp.id)
        self.assertEqual(len(stored.task_ids), 10)
